calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Vacated

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Aug-13-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Daniel T. Balmat, Esq., of Pacific Law Partners Llp, (510) 841-7777, for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff initiated this action against defendant State Farm Insurance Company arising out of an insurance claim for damaged personal property. The defendant served two sets of discovery on plaintiff: a set of special interrogatories and a Request For Admissions of Genuineness of Documents. Plaintiff did not respond to either discovery effort. Defendant sent one letter to plaintiff seeking responses to which it received no reply. The defendant then filed the two motions now before the Court: one for an order compelling responses to the special interrogatories and the second for an order affirming the genuineness of the documents attached to its Request for Admissions. Plaintiff has not responded to either motion. The defendant seeks sanctions in connection with each motion. Accordingly, for good cause shown, defendant's motion for an order compelling plaintiff to respond, without objections, to the set of special interrogatories served upon him is GRANTED. Similarly, defendant's motion for an order deeming genuine the documents attached to its Request for Admissions is GRANTED. Since defendant's efforts to secure these two orders did not require preparation of reply pleadings, and given their relative ease of preparation, sanctions are GRANTED against plaintiff, appearing in propria persona, in the amount of $800.00, payable within 10 days of the service date of the order referred to herein.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing decision and email it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)

DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff initiated this action against defendant State Farm Insurance Company arising out of an insurance claim for damaged personal property. The defendant served two sets of discovery on plaintiff: a set of special interrogatories and a Request For Admissions of Genuineness of Documents. Plaintiff did not respond to either discovery effort. Defendant sent one letter to plaintiff seeking responses to which it received no reply. The defendant then filed the two motions now before the Court: one for an order compelling responses to the special interrogatories and the second for an order affirming the genuineness of the documents attached to its Request for Admissions. Plaintiff has not responded to either motion. The defendant seeks sanctions in connection with each motion. Accordingly, for good cause shown, defendant's motion for an order compelling plaintiff to respond, without objections, to the set of special interrogatories served upon him is GRANTED. Similarly, defendant's motion for an order deeming genuine the documents attached to its Request for Admissions is GRANTED. Since defendant's efforts to secure these two orders did not require preparation of reply pleadings, and given their relative ease of preparation, sanctions are GRANTED against plaintiff, appearing in propria persona, in the amount of $800.00, payable within 10 days of the service date of the order referred to herein.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing decision and email it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (525/JPT)
